# Trump'tan Norveç'e 'Nobel Barış Ödülü'nü bana vermediniz' mektubu
![Donald Trump üzerinde montla konuşurken görülüyor. Suratı turuncu görünüyor, saçları uçuşuyor](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/640/cpsprodpb/70e8/live/48af6120-f599-11f0-a422-4ba8a094a8fa.png.webp)
Kaynak, Shawn Thew / EPA/Shutterstock
Fotoğraf altı yazısı, Donald Trump 16 Ocak'ta Beyaz Saray'da gazetecilere konuşuyor
8 saat önce
Okuma süresi: 2 dk
**ABD Başkanı Donald Trump, Norveç Başbakanı Jonas Gahr Store'ye gönderdiği imzalı bir mektupta, Nobel Barış Ödülü'nün kendisine verilmemesine tepki gösterdi.**
Grönland konusunda kendisine yapılan tansiyon azaltma çağrısıyla, Nobel'i ilişkilendirdi.
Norveç Başbakanı Store, bu mektubun, Finlandiya Devlet Başkanı Alexander Stubb ile birlikte Trump'a gönderdikleri mesaja yanıt olarak geldiğini söyledi.
Store Trump'a gönderdikleri mesajda, Grönland konusunda üçlü bir telefon görüşmesi talebinin yer aldığını söyledi.
Trump mektupta, "Sekizden fazla savaşı durdurmuş olmama rağmen ülkenizin bana Nobel Barış Ödülü vermeme kararı nedeniyle, artık sadece barış odaklı düşünmek zorunda hissetmiyorum" ifadelerini kullandı.
"Barış düşüncesi hala baskın olsa da artık ABD için neyin iyi ve doğru olduğunu da düşünüyorum" dedi.
ABD Başkanı, Grönland üzerinde "mutlak hakimiyet" kurmadığı müddetçe dünyanın güvenli bir yer olmayacağı yönündeki iddiasını sürdürüyor.
Avrupa başkentleri Grönland konusunda Beyaz Saray'dan gelen tehditleri bastırmaya çalışıyor.

![Nobel Barış Ödülü'nü sembolize eden altından madalya. Bir yuvarlak para gibi görünüyor. Fotoğraf çok yakından çekilmiş](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/640/cpsprodpb/4fe8/live/3c5c3550-f59a-11f0-b5f7-49f0357294ff.jpg.webp)
Kaynak, STR/NurPhoto via Getty Images
Fotoğraf altı yazısı, Nobel Barış Ödülü'nü sembolize eden madalya
ABD Başkanı Trump ikinci dönemine başlaması sonrası Nobel Barış Ödülü'nü kendisinin hak ettiğini iddia ediyordu.
Nobel komitesi ise 2025'teki ödülü Venezuelalı muhalif lider Maria Corina Machado'ya vermişti.
Venezuelalı muhalif lider Maria Corina Machado, 16 Ocak'ta Nobel Barış Ödülü madalyasını Beyaz Saray'da görüştüğü ABD Başkanı Donald Trump'a verdi.
Donald Trump sosyal medya hesabından yaptığı paylaşımda Machado'nun bu hareketinin "karşılıklı saygıya yönelik harika bir jest" olduğunu söylemişti.
## Ödül nasıl veriliyor?
Dünyanın en prestijli ödüllerinden biri olarak kabul edilen Nobel Barış Ödülü, İsveçli bilim insanı, iş insanı ve hayırsever Alfred Nobel'in oluşturduğu altı ödülden biri.
Kazananlar, Norveç Parlamentosu'nun seçtiği beş kişilik bir komite tarafından belirleniyor.
Ödül için 2025'te Trump da önemli bir aday olarak görülüyordu.
Trump da tekrar tekrar bu ödülü hakedenin kendisi olduğunu iddia etti.
Aralık'taki ödül duyurusu sonrası, Nobel Barış Komitesi Başkanı Jørgen Watne Frydnes'e baskının komitenin çalışmalarını etkileyip etkilemediği de soruldu.
Frydnes, buna Nobel Barış Ödülü'nün "uzun geçmişinde" komitenin buna benzer durumlarla karşılaştığını söyleyerek yanıt verdi.
"Kararımızı yalnızca Alfred Nobel'in çalışmalarına ve iradesine dayandırıyoruz" diye ekledi.

# Trump'tan Grönland çıkışı: 'Orayı almalıyız, koruyamıyorlar'
![Donald Trump, Başkan Yardımcısı JD Vance ve Dışişleri Bakanı Marco Rubio Grönland'de ABD bayrağı asıyor. Takım elbise giyen üç siyasetçinni yanında "Grönland, ABD toprağı, Kuruluş tarihi 2026" yazıyor. ](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/640/cpsprodpb/b553/live/d55e98e0-f5ce-11f0-a32c-ff5834b4c89e.jpg.webp)
Kaynak, Truth Social/Donald Trump
Fotoğraf altı yazısı, ABD Başkanı Donald Trump, sosyal medya hesabından Grönland'e ABD bayrağı diktiği bir görsel paylaştı.**Haber bilgisi**
  *     * Yazan, BBC News Türkçe
    * Bildirdiği yer,  Londra
  * 19 Ocak 2026
Güncelleme bir saat önce
  * Okuma süresi: 4 dk


**ABD Başkanı Donald Trump, Grönland ile ilgili son açıklamasında "orayı almalıyız, [Danimarka] orayı koruyamıyor" dedi.**
Trump 19 Ocak'ta yaptığı açıklamada ayrıca NATO müttefiklerinin ABD'nin Grönland üzerinde hak iddia etmesine karşı "çok diretmeyeceklerini" öne sürdü ve Avrupa ülkelerinin adaya yaptığı sevkiyatın "askeri olmadığını" söyledi.
ABD başkanı "NATO Danimarka'yı 20 yıldır uyarıyor. Rus tehdidi konusunda uyarıyorlar, Sadece Rusya değil aynı zamanda Çin de var" dedi ve şu ifadeleri kullandı:
"Neler olacağına bakalım. Şöyle ifade edeyim: İlginç bir Davos olacak."
Trump kendi sosyal medya platformu Truth Social üzerinden yaptığı bir paylaşımda Başkan Yardımcısı JD Vance ve Dışişleri Bakanı Marco Rubio ile birlikte Grönland'e ABD bayrağı diktikleri bir görsel yer aldı.
Başka bir paylaşımda ise Fransa Cumhurbaşkanı Emmanuel Macron'un kendisine gönderdiği bir mesajı paylaştı.
Mesajda Macron'un Trump'a "Grönland ile ilgili ne yaptığını anlamıyorum" dediği ve Davos zirvesinden sonra bir G7 toplantısı organize etmeyi teklif ettiği görülüyor.

[DEM Parti grup toplantısını Suriye sınırındaki Nusaybin'e aldı](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/articles/c0erxy74zrlo)
  * ![Fotoğraftaki göstericiler polis barikatında durdurulmuş durumda. Kare yakından çekilmiş ve en önde bir kadın polisi itmeye çalışıyor. Arkada da çok sayıda kişi var.](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/660/cpsprodpb/555f/live/47c0ada0-f594-11f0-9549-57623bb458e7.jpg.webp)
[Diyarbakır ve Van'daki Suriye protestolarında çok sayıda gözaltı](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/articles/c1m73yedry3o)

[Virgin Adaları: Danimarka 100 yıl önce bu takımadayı neden ABD'ye sattı?](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/articles/clyg0nq1zq0o)


Haberin sonu
Reuters haber ajansına konuşan Fransız kaynaklar mesajın Macron'a ait olduğunu doğruladı. 
Trump bunun yanı sıra NATO Genel Sekreteri Mark Rutte'den de bir mesaj paylaştı.
Mesajda "Grönland konusunda bir yol bulmakta kararlıyım" ifadeleri yer alıyor.
## Vergi tehdidi
ABD Başkanı Donald Trump bu paylaşımlarından önce Grönland'ı ilhak etme açıklamalarına karşı çıkan Avrupa ülkelerine gümrük vergisi uygulama tehdidini yinelemişti.
NBC televizyonuna konuşan Trump, ek vergi tehdidinin "yüzde 100" ciddi olduğunu söylemişti.
Grönland konusunda askeri güç kullanıp kullanmayacağı sorusunaysa Trump, "Yorum yok" yanıtını vermişti.
ABD başkanı, Avrupa'nın Rusya-Ukrayna savaşına odaklanması gerektiğini de savunmuştu.
Grönland başlıklı gelişmeler Davos'taki Dünya Ekonomik Forumu gündeminin de ön sırasında yer alıyor.
Burada BBC'ye konuşan IBM'nin başkan yardımcısı Gary Cohn, Trump'ın açıklamalarını Grönland'daki kritik minerallerle ilişkilendirdi.
Cohn, ilk döneminde Trump'a ekonomik konularda danışmanlık yapmış bir isim.
Uzman, Trump'ın Grönland hakkındaki son açıklamalarının "bir müzakerenin parçası olabileceğini" öne sürdü.
Trump'ın Çarşamba günü Davos'ta konuşması bekleniyor. ABD başkanı 2020'den beri ilk kez zirveye katılacak. 
## 'Avrupa şantaja boyun eğmeyecek'
İngiltere Başbakanı Keir Starmer 19 Ocak'taki konuşmasında Trump'ın yeni gümrük vergileri açıklamasının "tamamen yanlış olduğunu" söyledi.
Starmer "Grönland'ın geleceğine yalnızca Grönland halkı ve Danimarka Krallığı karar verebilir" diye konuştu.
Trump NBC'ye verdiği röportajda, 1 Şubat'tan itibaren ABD'ye İngiltere'den gönderilen "her türlü mala" yüzde 10 ek vergi uygulanacağını duyurdu.
1 Haziran'dan itibaren ise gümrük vergisinin %25'e çıkacağını kaydetti.
Trump'ın gümrük vergisiyle tehdit ettiği di

url: https://www.bbc.com/turkce/articles/cj6wn3y3555o#:~:text=Türkiye, bu bölgedeki Kürt silahlı,Şam yönetimini "yalnız bırakmayacağını" vurguladı.

# Suriye'de son durum ne?
![ Izz Aldien Alqasem/Anadolu via Getty Images](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/640/cpsprodpb/7d6b/live/01938260-f5dc-11f0-9163-bde404de4781.jpg.webp)
Kaynak, Izz Aldien Alqasem/ Anadolu Ajansı/ Getty Images
**Haber bilgisi**
  *     * Yazan, BBC News Türkçe
    * Bildirdiği yer,  Londra
  * 19 Ocak 2026
Güncelleme 5 dakika önce
  * Okuma süresi: 2 dk


**Kürt yetkililer, Suriye lideri Ahmed Şara ile SDG komutanı Mazlum Abdi arasındaki görüşmenin olumsuz sonuçlandığına ilişkin açıklamalar yapıyor.**
Franız haber ajansı AFP bir Kürt yetkilinin bu yönde bilgi verdiğini aktardı.
Irak Kürdistan Bölgesel Yönetimi'ne yakınlığıyla bilinen Rudaw'ın haberine göre de Demokratik Birlik Partisi'nin (PYD) kıdemli üyesi Foza Alyusuf, Abdi ile Şara arasında gerçekleşen "ateşkes" ve "entegrasyon" görüşmesinin "olumsuz sonuçlandığını" söyledi.
Suriye yönetiminden henüz bu açıklamaları doğrulayan bir değerlendirme yapılmadı.
Foza Alyusuf'un, "Şam 'her şeyinizi teslim edeceksiniz ve durum 2011'dekinden öncesine dönecek' diyor. Hiçbir şekilde varlığımızı kabul etmeye yanaşmıyor" dediği aktarıldı.
Şam'dan ayrılan Abdi'yle durumu değerlendireceklerini söyleyen Alyusuf, Kürt güçlerine genel seferberlik ilan etmeleri çağrısında da bulundu.
ABD Başkanı Donald Trump ile Suriye'nin geçiş dönemi Devlet Başkanı Ahmed Şara arasında da bir telefon görüşmesi gerçekleştirildi.

[Diyarbakır ve Van'daki Suriye protestolarında çok sayıda gözaltı](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/articles/c1m73yedry3o)
  * ![Mavi sularla çevrili bir koyda demirlemiş beyaz tekneler, önünde beyaz binalarla bezeli yeşil, ağaçlarla kaplı tepeler yer alıyor.](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/660/cpsprodpb/3b9a/live/863e7c80-f556-11f0-a712-a38af32eabc0.jpg.webp)
[Virgin Adaları: Danimarka 100 yıl önce bu takımadayı neden ABD'ye sattı?](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/articles/clyg0nq1zq0o)


Haberin sonu
Şam yönetimine göre görüşmede, Kürtlerin haklarının birleşik bir Suriye içerisinde korunması gerektiğinin üzerinde duruldu.
Rakka'daki AFP muhabiri ağır çatışmaların yaşandığını bildirdi. Yine ajansa bilgi veren bir Şam yetkilisi çatışmaların durduğunu açıkladı.
PKK'ya yakın Fırat Haber Ajansı'nda (ANF) SDG'nin direniş çağrısı yaptığı aktarılıyor.
AB Dış Politika Yüksek Temsilcisi Kaja Kallas, Suriye'deki "tüm askeri faaliyetlerin" durdurulması çağrısı yaptı. 
## Bu noktaya kadar neler yaşandı?
18 Ocak'ta duyurusu yapılan ateşkesin iki haftadır süren çatışmaları sona erdirmesi bekleniyordu.
SDG komutanı Mazlum Abdi çatışmaların kendilerine "dayatıldığını" ve anlaşmayı "akan kanı durdurmak için" kabul ettiklerini ifade etmişti.
![İki ağır zırhlı askeri aracın arasında 4 silahlı Suriye askeri var. Yol kapatılmış durumda. Hava açık ama soğuk olduğu giysilerden anlaşılıyor.](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/640/cpsprodpb/c935/live/9d33fcc0-f589-11f0-b5f7-49f0357294ff.png.webp)
Kaynak, Mahmoud Hassano / Reuters
Fotoğraf altı yazısı, Rakka cezaevi yakınındaki Suriye ordusu askerleri - 19 Ocak 2025
SDG güçlerinin, Deyrizor ve Rakka'dan, Haseke'ye çekilmesi bekleniyordu.
  * [14 maddelik anlaşmada neler vardı?](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/articles/c1kl0eevy3jo)


Suriye'nin geçiş dönemi Devlet Başkanı Ahmed Şara ise, anlaşmanın Suriye'nin "bölünmüşlük halini sona erdirerek birlik ve ilerleme durumuna geçmesini" sağlayacağını umduğunu söylemişti.
Türkiye, bu bölgedeki Kürt silahlı güçleri, PKK'nın Suriye'deki kolu ve "terör örgütü" olarak kabul ediyor.
Cumhurbaşkanı Recep Tayyip Erdoğan 19 Ocak'ta yaptığı açıklamada Türkiye'nin Şam yönetimini "yalnız bırakmayacağını" vurguladı.
"Ateşkes ve Tam Entegrasyon Anlaşması'nın gerekleri süratle yerine getirilmeli, hiç kimse bir kere daha yanlış hesap yapmamalıdır" dedi.
20 Ocak'ta Dışişleri Bakanı Hakan Fidan, Ankara'da ABD'nin Ankara Büyükelçisi ve Suriye Özel Temsilcisi Tom Barrack'la görüştü.
## İlgili haberler
  * ![Suriye hükümet güçleri Rakka şehrinin yakınlarında SDG askeri üssünün önünde ağır silahlarla](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/660/cpsprodpb/9e8

url: https://www.bbc.com/turkce/articles/c5y2p220j9yo#:~:text=ABD Başkanı Donald Trump, Grönland,Trump 19 Ocak'ta yaptığı açıklamada

# Trump'tan Grönland çıkışı: 'Orayı almalıyız, koruyamıyorlar'
![Donald Trump, Başkan Yardımcısı JD Vance ve Dışişleri Bakanı Marco Rubio Grönland'de ABD bayrağı asıyor. Takım elbise giyen üç siyasetçinni yanında "Grönland, ABD toprağı, Kuruluş tarihi 2026" yazıyor. ](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/640/cpsprodpb/b553/live/d55e98e0-f5ce-11f0-a32c-ff5834b4c89e.jpg.webp)
Kaynak, Truth Social/Donald Trump
Fotoğraf altı yazısı, ABD Başkanı Donald Trump, sosyal medya hesabından Grönland'e ABD bayrağı diktiği bir görsel paylaştı.**Haber bilgisi**
  *     * Yazan, BBC News Türkçe
    * Bildirdiği yer,  Londra
  * 19 Ocak 2026
Güncelleme bir saat önce
  * Okuma süresi: 4 dk


**ABD Başkanı Donald Trump, Grönland ile ilgili son açıklamasında "orayı almalıyız, [Danimarka] orayı koruyamıyor" dedi.**
Trump 19 Ocak'ta yaptığı açıklamada ayrıca NATO müttefiklerinin ABD'nin Grönland üzerinde hak iddia etmesine karşı "çok diretmeyeceklerini" öne sürdü ve Avrupa ülkelerinin adaya yaptığı sevkiyatın "askeri olmadığını" söyledi.
ABD başkanı "NATO Danimarka'yı 20 yıldır uyarıyor. Rus tehdidi konusunda uyarıyorlar, Sadece Rusya değil aynı zamanda Çin de var" dedi ve şu ifadeleri kullandı:
"Neler olacağına bakalım. Şöyle ifade edeyim: İlginç bir Davos olacak."
Trump kendi sosyal medya platformu Truth Social üzerinden yaptığı bir paylaşımda Başkan Yardımcısı JD Vance ve Dışişleri Bakanı Marco Rubio ile birlikte Grönland'e ABD bayrağı diktikleri bir görsel yer aldı.
Başka bir paylaşımda ise Fransa Cumhurbaşkanı Emmanuel Macron'un kendisine gönderdiği bir mesajı paylaştı.
Mesajda Macron'un Trump'a "Grönland ile ilgili ne yaptığını anlamıyorum" dediği ve Davos zirvesinden sonra bir G7 toplantısı organize etmeyi teklif ettiği görülüyor.
[Haberin başlığını atlayın ve okumaya devam edin](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/articles/c5y2p220j9yo#end-of-recommendations)
**En çok okunanlar**
  * ![ Izz Aldien Alqasem/Anadolu via Getty Images](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/660/cpsprodpb/7d6b/live/01938260-f5dc-11f0-9163-bde404de4781.jpg.webp)
[Suriye'de son durum ne?](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/articles/cj6wn3y3555o)
  * ![DEM Parti Eş Genel Başkanı Tuncer Bakırhan, kürsüde konuşma yapıyor. Arkasında parti amblemi görülüyor.](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/660/cpsprodpb/6536/live/3bc0e7e0-f567-11f0-aafe-33121222cc9f.jpg.webp)
[DEM Parti grup toplantısını Suriye sınırındaki Nusaybin'e aldı](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/articles/c0erxy74zrlo)
  * ![Fotoğraftaki göstericiler polis barikatında durdurulmuş durumda. Kare yakından çekilmiş ve en önde bir kadın polisi itmeye çalışıyor. Arkada da çok sayıda kişi var.](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/660/cpsprodpb/555f/live/47c0ada0-f594-11f0-9549-57623bb458e7.jpg.webp)
[Diyarbakır ve Van'daki Suriye protestolarında çok sayıda gözaltı](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/articles/c1m73yedry3o)
  * ![Mavi sularla çevrili bir koyda demirlemiş beyaz tekneler, önünde beyaz binalarla bezeli yeşil, ağaçlarla kaplı tepeler yer alıyor.](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/660/cpsprodpb/3b9a/live/863e7c80-f556-11f0-a712-a38af32eabc0.jpg.webp)
[Virgin Adaları: Danimarka 100 yıl önce bu takımadayı neden ABD'ye sattı?](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/articles/clyg0nq1zq0o)


Haberin sonu
Reuters haber ajansına konuşan Fransız kaynaklar mesajın Macron'a ait olduğunu doğruladı. 
Trump bunun yanı sıra NATO Genel Sekreteri Mark Rutte'den de bir mesaj paylaştı.
Mesajda "Grönland konusunda bir yol bulmakta kararlıyım" ifadeleri yer alıyor.
## Vergi tehdidi
ABD Başkanı Donald Trump bu paylaşımlarından önce Grönland'ı ilhak etme açıklamalarına karşı çıkan Avrupa ülkelerine gümrük vergisi uygulama tehdidini yinelemişti.
NBC televizyonuna konuşan Trump, ek vergi tehdidinin "yüzde 100" ciddi olduğunu söylemişti.
Grönland konusunda askeri güç kullanıp kullanmayacağı sorusunaysa Trump, "Yorum yok" yanıtını vermişti.
ABD başkanı, Avrupa'nın Rusya-Ukrayna savaşına odaklanması gerektiğini de savunmuştu.
Grönland başlıklı gelişmeler Davos'taki Dünya Ekonomik Forumu gündeminin de ön sırasında yer alıyor.
Burada BBC'ye konuşan IBM'nin başkan yardımcısı Gary Cohn, Trump'ın açıklamalarını Grönland'daki kritik minerallerle ilişkilendirdi.
Cohn, ilk döneminde Trump'a ekonomik konularda danışmanlık yapmış bir isim.
Uzman, Trump'ın Grönland hakkındaki son açıklamalarının "bir müzakerenin parçası olabileceğini" öne sürdü.
Trump'ın Çarşamba günü Davos'ta konuşması bekleniyor. ABD başkanı 2020'den beri ilk kez zirveye katılacak. 
## 'Avrupa şantaja boyun eğmeyecek'
İngiltere Başbakanı Keir Starmer 19 Ocak'taki konuşmasında Trump'ın yeni gümrük vergileri açıklamasının "tamamen yanlış olduğunu" söyledi.
Starmer "Grönland'ın geleceğine yalnızca Grönland halkı ve Danimarka Krallığı karar verebilir" diye konuştu.
Trump NBC'ye verdiği röportajda, 1 Şubat'tan itibaren ABD'ye İngiltere'den gönderilen "her türlü mala" yüzde 10 ek vergi uygulanacağını duyurdu.
1 Haziran'dan itibaren ise gümrük vergisinin %25'e çıkacağını kaydetti.
Trump'ın gümrük vergisiyle tehdit ettiği di

url: https://www.bbc.com/turkce/articles/cdexgpdjw38o#:~:text=Cumhurbaşkanı Recep Tayyip Erdoğan 19,daha yanlış hesap yapmamalıdır" dedi.

# IŞİD'lilerin tutulduğu hapishanelerde son durum ne?
![Rakka'daki El Aktan Cezaevi önünde kırmızı bereli bir Suriye ordusu askeri var. Arkada fonda cezaevi görünüyor.](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/640/cpsprodpb/315f/live/ba62ebf0-f596-11f0-b5f7-49f0357294ff.jpg.webp)
Kaynak, Bakr ALkasem / AFP via Getty Images
Fotoğraf altı yazısı, Rakka'daki El Aktan Cezaevi önünde bir Suriye ordusu askeri var. Cezaevinin kontrolüyse SDG'deydi. - 19 Ocak 2025**Haber bilgisi**
  *     * Yazan, BBC News Türkçe
    * Unvan,  Londra
  * 19 Ocak 2026
Güncelleme bir saat önce
  * Okuma süresi: 3 dk


**Suriye'de geçiş yönetimine bağlı İçişleri Bakanlığı 20 Ocak'ta, IŞİD'e mensup olduğu iddia edilen yaklaşık 120 mahkumun, Suriye Demokratik Güçleri'nin (SDG) kontrolündeki Şaddadi cezaevinden kaçtığını açıkladı.**
SDG daha önce IŞİD'in yaklaşık 1500 üyesinin cezaevinden kaçtığını iddia etmişti. 
Suriye İçişleri Bakanlığı, kaçış operasyonunun ardından Suriye ordusu ve özel kuvvetlerinin Şaddadi'ye girdiğini belirtti.
Bakanlık, güvenlik güçlerinin şehirde ve çevresinde yapılan aramaların ardından firarilerden 81'ini yakaladığını ve takip çalışmalarının devam ettiğini ekledi.
SDG ve Şam yönetimi söz konusu kaçışlarla ilgili birbirlerini suçluyor.
SDG'nin basın ofisi, Ayn İsa, Şeddadi ve Rakka'da, ordu güçleriyle çatışmalar yaşandığını duyurdu.
Haseke'nin güneyindeki, binlerce IŞİD'li mahkumun bulunduğu Şeddadi Hapishanesi çevresindeki hareketlilikle ilgili iki taraftan karşılıklı iddialar geldi. 
Suriye ordusu SDG'yi IŞİD'lileri "serbest bırakmakla" suçlarken, Kürt güçlerin öncülüğündeki grup, orduyu hapishaneye "saldırmakla" itham etti.
[Haberin başlığını atlayın ve okumaya devam edin](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/articles/cdexgpdjw38o#end-of-recommendations)
**En çok okunanlar**
  * ![ Izz Aldien Alqasem/Anadolu via Getty Images](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/660/cpsprodpb/7d6b/live/01938260-f5dc-11f0-9163-bde404de4781.jpg.webp)
[Suriye'de son durum ne?](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/articles/cj6wn3y3555o)
  * ![DEM Parti Eş Genel Başkanı Tuncer Bakırhan, kürsüde konuşma yapıyor. Arkasında parti amblemi görülüyor.](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/660/cpsprodpb/6536/live/3bc0e7e0-f567-11f0-aafe-33121222cc9f.jpg.webp)
[DEM Parti grup toplantısını Suriye sınırındaki Nusaybin'e aldı](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/articles/c0erxy74zrlo)
  * ![Fotoğraftaki göstericiler polis barikatında durdurulmuş durumda. Kare yakından çekilmiş ve en önde bir kadın polisi itmeye çalışıyor. Arkada da çok sayıda kişi var.](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/660/cpsprodpb/555f/live/47c0ada0-f594-11f0-9549-57623bb458e7.jpg.webp)
[Diyarbakır ve Van'daki Suriye protestolarında çok sayıda gözaltı](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/articles/c1m73yedry3o)
  * ![Mavi sularla çevrili bir koyda demirlemiş beyaz tekneler, önünde beyaz binalarla bezeli yeşil, ağaçlarla kaplı tepeler yer alıyor.](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/660/cpsprodpb/3b9a/live/863e7c80-f556-11f0-a712-a38af32eabc0.jpg.webp)
[Virgin Adaları: Danimarka 100 yıl önce bu takımadayı neden ABD'ye sattı?](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/articles/clyg0nq1zq0o)


Haberin sonu
Şam yönetimi, Şeddadi cezaevi yönetiminin güçlerine devredilmediğini açıkladı.
BBC Türkçe bu iddiaları bağımsız olarak teyit edemedi. 
Rudaw'ın açıklamalarını aktardığı Suriye Demokratik Güçleri (SDG) Sözcüsü Farhad Şami, 1.500 IŞİD'linin Şaddadi hapishanesinden kaçtığını iddia etti.
ABD önderliğindeki koalisyona ait askeri üssün iki kilometre uzakta olmasına karşın yardım çağrılarına karşılık vermediği de öne sürüldü.
Suriye'nin resmi haber ajansı SANA'ya açıklama yapan Suriye ordu komutanlığı, serbest bırakıldığını iddia ettikleri kişilerin yakalanması için çalışma başlattıklarını kaydetti.
SANA'ya göre Şeddadi şehrinde sokağa çıkma yasağı da ilan edildi.
Ajansın haberine göre Suriye hükümeti SDG'yi "siyasi baskı taktiği" olarak IŞİD militanlarının hapishanelerden "kaçmasına izin vermemesi" veya "serbest bırakmaması" konusunda uyaran bir açıklama yayımladı.
"Suriye devleti bu tür eylemleri savaş suçu ve Suriye'nin ve tüm bölgenin güvenliğini tehdit eden terörizmle doğrudan işbirliği olarak değerlendirecek" denildi.
![Suriye'de son durumu gösteren harita](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/640/cpsprodpb/45c7/live/d7d21b10-f538-11f0-a422-4ba8a094a8fa.jpg.webp)
## İletişim Başkanlığı'ndan açıklama
İletişim Başkanlığı'na bağlı Dezenformasyonla Mücadele Merkezi'nden yapılan açıklamada ise "Suriye'de yaşanan süreçte Türkiye'nin desteği ile Suriye ordusu tarafından DEAŞ'lı teröristlerin serbest bırakıldığı iddiası tamamen asılsızdır" ifadeleri yer aldı.
Açıklamada, Türkiye'nin IŞİD'e karşı "bölgede en güçlü mücadeleyi veren ülke olduğunun bilindiği" dile getirildi: 
"Kamuoyunun ve asırlardır aynı coğrafyayı, birçok müşterek değeri paylaştığımız dost, kardeş ve komşularımızın bu tarz asılsız iddialara itibar etmemesi önemle rica olunur."
## SDG açıklaması
SDG tarafından yapılan bir diğer açıklamada, Rakka'da IŞİD'lilerin tutulduğu El Aktan Hapishanesi'nin çe

Rudaw'ın açıklamalarını aktardığı Suriye Demokratik Güçleri (SDG) Sözcüsü Farhad Şami, 1.500 IŞİD'linin Şaddadi hapishanesinden kaçtığını iddia etti.
ABD önderliğindeki koalisyona ait askeri üssün iki kilometre uzakta olmasına karşın yardım çağrılarına karşılık vermediği de öne sürüldü.
Suriye'nin resmi haber ajansı SANA'ya açıklama yapan Suriye ordu komutanlığı, serbest bırakıldığını iddia ettikleri kişilerin yakalanması için çalışma başlattıklarını kaydetti.
SANA'ya göre Şeddadi şehrinde sokağa çıkma yasağı da ilan edildi.
Ajansın haberine göre Suriye hükümeti SDG'yi "siyasi baskı taktiği" olarak IŞİD militanlarının hapishanelerden "kaçmasına izin vermemesi" veya "serbest bırakmaması" konusunda uyaran bir açıklama yayımladı.
"Suriye devleti bu tür eylemleri savaş suçu ve Suriye'nin ve tüm bölgenin güvenliğini tehdit eden terörizmle doğrudan işbirliği olarak değerlendirecek" denildi.
![Suriye'de son durumu gösteren harita](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/640/cpsprodpb/45c7/live/d7d21b10-f538-11f0-a422-4ba8a094a8fa.jpg.webp)
## İletişim Başkanlığı'ndan açıklama
İletişim Başkanlığı'na bağlı Dezenformasyonla Mücadele Merkezi'nden yapılan açıklamada ise "Suriye'de yaşanan süreçte Türkiye'nin desteği ile Suriye ordusu tarafından DEAŞ'lı teröristlerin serbest bırakıldığı iddiası tamamen asılsızdır" ifadeleri yer aldı.
Açıklamada, Türkiye'nin IŞİD'e karşı "bölgede en güçlü mücadeleyi veren ülke olduğunun bilindiği" dile getirildi: 
"Kamuoyunun ve asırlardır aynı coğrafyayı, birçok müşterek değeri paylaştığımız dost, kardeş ve komşularımızın bu tarz asılsız iddialara itibar etmemesi önemle rica olunur."
## SDG açıklaması
SDG tarafından yapılan bir diğer açıklamada, Rakka'da IŞİD'lilerin tutulduğu El Aktan Hapishanesi'nin çe

# Suriye'de son durum ne?
![ Izz Aldien Alqasem/Anadolu via Getty Images](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/640/cpsprodpb/7d6b/live/01938260-f5dc-11f0-9163-bde404de4781.jpg.webp)

**Kürt yetkililer, Suriye lideri Ahmed Şara ile SDG komutanı Mazlum Abdi arasındaki görüşmenin olumsuz sonuçlandığına ilişkin açıklamalar yapıyor.**
Franız haber ajansı AFP bir Kürt yetkilinin bu yönde bilgi verdiğini aktardı.
Irak Kürdistan Bölgesel Yönetimi'ne yakınlığıyla bilinen Rudaw'ın haberine göre de Demokratik Birlik Partisi'nin (PYD) kıdemli üyesi Foza Alyusuf, Abdi ile Şara arasında gerçekleşen "ateşkes" ve "entegrasyon" görüşmesinin "olumsuz sonuçlandığını" söyledi.
Suriye yönetiminden henüz bu açıklamaları doğrulayan bir değerlendirme yapılmadı.
Foza Alyusuf'un, "Şam 'her şeyinizi teslim edeceksiniz ve durum 2011'dekinden öncesine dönecek' diyor. Hiçbir şekilde varlığımızı kabul etmeye yanaşmıyor" dediği aktarıldı.
Şam'dan ayrılan Abdi'yle durumu değerlendireceklerini söyleyen Alyusuf, Kürt güçlerine genel seferberlik ilan etmeleri çağrısında da bulundu.
ABD Başkanı Donald Trump ile Suriye'nin geçiş dönemi Devlet Başkanı Ahmed Şara arasında da bir telefon görüşmesi gerçekleştirildi.

Haberin sonu
Şam yönetimine göre görüşmede, Kürtlerin haklarının birleşik bir Suriye içerisinde korunması gerektiğinin üzerinde duruldu.
Rakka'daki AFP muhabiri ağır çatışmaların yaşandığını bildirdi. Yine ajansa bilgi veren bir Şam yetkilisi çatışmaların durduğunu açıkladı.
PKK'ya yakın Fırat Haber Ajansı'nda (ANF) SDG'nin direniş çağrısı yaptığı aktarılıyor.
AB Dış Politika Yüksek Temsilcisi Kaja Kallas, Suriye'deki "tüm askeri faaliyetlerin" durdurulması çağrısı yaptı. 
## Bu noktaya kadar neler yaşandı?
18 Ocak'ta duyurusu yapılan ateşkesin iki haftadır süren çatışmaları sona erdirmesi bekleniyordu.
SDG komutanı Mazlum Abdi çatışmaların kendilerine "dayatıldığını" ve anlaşmayı "akan kanı durdurmak için" kabul ettiklerini ifade etmişti.
![İki ağır zırhlı askeri aracın arasında 4 silahlı Suriye askeri var. Yol kapatılmış durumda. Hava açık ama soğuk olduğu giysilerden anlaşılıyor.](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/ws/640/cpsprodpb/c935/live/9d33fcc0-f589-11f0-b5f7-49f0357294ff.png.webp)

  * [14 maddelik anlaşmada neler vardı?](https://www.bbc.com/turkce/articles/c1kl0eevy3jo)


Suriye'nin geçiş dönemi Devlet Başkanı Ahmed Şara ise, anlaşmanın Suriye'nin "bölünmüşlük halini sona erdirerek birlik ve ilerleme durumuna geçmesini" sağlayacağını umduğunu söylemişti.
Türkiye, bu bölgedeki Kürt silahlı güçleri, PKK'nın Suriye'deki kolu ve "terör örgütü" olarak kabul ediyor.
Cumhurbaşkanı Recep Tayyip Erdoğan 19 Ocak'ta yaptığı açıklamada Türkiye'nin Şam yönetimini "yalnız bırakmayacağını" vurguladı.
"Ateşkes ve Tam Entegrasyon Anlaşması'nın gerekleri süratle yerine getirilmeli, hiç kimse bir kere daha yanlış hesap yapmamalıdır" dedi.
20 Ocak'ta Dışişleri Bakanı Hakan Fidan, Ankara'da ABD'nin Ankara Büyükelçisi ve Suriye Özel Temsilcisi Tom Barrack'la görüştü.

Tensions between the Syrian government and the Kurdish-led Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) have been escalating, days after they announced a ceasefire, [which is being severely tested](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/liveblog/2026/1/20/syria-fighting-live-ceasefire-under-duress-as-govt-moves-into-hasakah) by renewed fighting, involving the withdrawal of the latter’s forces from areas west of the Euphrates River.
Talks in Damascus between Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa and SDF leader Mazloum Abdi, also known as Mazloum Kobani, have faltered, and Abdi has returned to the northeast.

Contention and a blame game have surrounded the issues of ISIL (ISIS) prisoners who escaped from al-Shaddadi prison during the fighting between the army and the SDF. Syria’s Ministry of Interior said on Tuesday that 130 of 200 ISIL escapees had been recaptured.
The ministry accused the SDF of releasing ISIL fighters from the prison in the northeastern city of al-Shaddadi as a form of “political and security blackmail”. The army said it deliberately bypassed al‑Shaddadi prison in line with an agreement that the SDF would later hand control of the facility to authorities in Damascus.
The SDF has blamed the Syrian army for the prison break, saying it “lost control” of the jail after an attack by tribal fighters affiliated with the army.
SDF commander Fawza Youssef on Tuesday blamed al-Sharaa’s government for failing to uphold the deal.
“There is no political will on the part of the government to implement a ceasefire,” he told Al Jazeera. “If the violations and attacks continue, the SDF cannot be disarmed.”
The [agreement reached on Sunday](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/1/18/syria-announces-ceasefire-agreement-with-kurd-led-sdf-after-heavy-fighting) between al-Sharaa and Abdi stipulates the withdrawal of the SDF from Raqqa and Deir Az Zor, parts of which are east of the Euphrates, within a month.
Advertisement 
A spokesperson for the Interior Ministry accused the SDF of “trying to deflect blame” as it faced internal divisions.
“We prefer peaceful solutions, but all options are open,” the spokesperson told Al Jazeera.

The deal included the SDF’s withdrawal from the area around the al-Aqtan prison. As it appeared on the verge of collapse, Al Jazeera correspondents on Tuesday reported that the Syrian army had begun artillery shelling around the prison and the SDF’s 17th Division headquarters in Raqqa.
Local sources in Hasakah province in the northeast said the army reached the Panorama intersection, located at the southern entrance to the provincial capital, also called Hasakah.
The Interior Ministry said it was ready to take over the management and security of ISIL prisons in Hasakah in accordance with international standards.
Information Minister Hamza Mustafa said the army secured the city of al-Shaddadi in the Hasakah countryside after taking control of the prison located there.
## Taking control of ISIL prisons
The SDF, the United States-backed force that fought ISIL in Syria, controls more than a dozen prisons in the northeast, where about 9,000 ISIL members have been held for years without trial.
While al-Sharaa’s government has pledged to reunify Syria after nearly 14 years of war, the SDF has repeatedly underlined al-Sharaa’s previous affiliation with Hayat Tahrir al-Sham (HTS), formerly part of al-Qaeda.
In a statement on Monday, the SDF referred to the government as “ISIS sympat

## At least 81 ISIL fighters re-arrested after escape from prison in Hasakah
Syria’s Interior Ministry has announced the recapture of 81 out of 120 ISIL (ISIS) detainees who escaped from the al-Shaddadi Prison in northeastern Hasakah governate.
“The Syrian army and special operations units affiliated with the Interior Ministry entered the city of al-Shaddadi,” it said in a statement.
“Army and Interior Ministry forces conducted a comprehensive search and sweep operation in and around the city of al-Shaddadi. During the operation, 81 ISIS terrorists were apprehended. The search for the remaining ISIS members continues.”
The ministry in a statement to Al Jazeera on Monday said 130 out 200 escapees had been apprehended. It’s unclear why the figures changed.

## Trump tells NATO partners ‘we are totally in line on Syria’
US President Donald Trump has shared two messages he exchanged with French President Emmanuel Macron and NATO chief Mark Rutte on his platform Truth Social.
In what Trump said was a “Note from President Emmanuel Macron of France” the message said, “We are totally in line on Syria.”
Trump and Macron have both expressed concern about the unfolding situation in Syria, but have expressed support for maintaining the country’s unity.
In a separate image Trump shared, he thanked Rutte after NATO’s boss described Trump’s achievements in Syria as “incredible”.

## SDF says it repelled 2 attacks by government forces in Kobani
The SDF says it repelled two attacks in the early hours of Tuesday carried out by government forces near the Syrian Kurdish town of Kobani.
It said in a statement that armoured military vehicles and tanks were destroyed and casualties were reported.
Fighters in the “south of Kobani witnessed two consecutive attack attempts by Damascus-affiliated factions, which were courageously and competently repelled by our forces”, it said.
“The confrontations on that front resulted in the destruction of a large number of their military vehicles and armoured vehicles, and the deaths of those inside them.”
The SDF took over control of Kobani after the armed group ISIL (ISIS) was driven from the city.

## Washington in unique position to end crisis with strong ties to both sides
[![](https://www.aljazeera.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Ayman-Oghanna-1768832707.jpg?resize=96%2C96&quality=80)](https://www.aljazeera.com/author/ayman_oghanna_2013318211859654188)
By [Ayman Oghanna](https://www.aljazeera.com/author/ayman_oghanna_2013318211859654188)
Reporting from Damascus, Syria
After the meeting with SDF commander Abdi, the Syrian president’s office released a statement saying there was a phone call between Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa and US President Donald Trump.
In the call they discussed the developments in Syria and, according to the office, Trump reaffirmed his commitment to Syrian national unity and “combating terrorism”.
Now of all the foreign players, the United States is in a unique position to end this crisis. It has enjoyed very close ties with both the SDF and Damascus.
In terms of the SDF, for more than a decade Washington has equipped, trained, and fought alongside the SDF against the armed group ISIL. The US still has 900 troops in SDF-controlled areas.
But in the past year, we’ve seen a real strengthening of ties between the new government in Damascus and Washington. Last November, al-Sharaa was in DC. He went to the White House where he was feted and praised by Trump.
Before the ceasefire discussions we spoke to Syrian Kurds in the northeast who said they were worried about the US’s growing ties with Damascus, and had fears the US may decide – in fighting between the two – which relationship is more important.

## At least 81 ISIL fighters re-arrested after escape from prison in Hasakah
Syria’s Interior Ministry has announced the recapture of 81 out of 120 ISIL (ISIS) detainees who escaped from the al-Shaddadi Prison in northeastern Hasakah governate.
“The Syrian army and special operations units affiliated with the Interior Ministry entered the city of al-Shaddadi,” it said in a statement.
“Army and Interior Ministry forces conducted a comprehensive search and sweep operation in and around the city of al-Shaddadi. During the operation, 81 ISIS terrorists were apprehended. The search for the remaining ISIS members continues.”
The ministry in a statement to Al Jazeera on Monday said 130 out 200 escapees had been apprehended. It’s unclear why the figures changed.

## Trump tells NATO partners ‘we are totally in line on Syria’
US President Donald Trump has shared two messages he exchanged with French President Emmanuel Macron and NATO chief Mark Rutte on his platform Truth Social.
In what Trump said was a “Note from President Emmanuel Macron of France” the message said, “We are totally in line on Syria.”
Trump and Macron have both expressed concern about the unfolding situation in Syria, but have expressed support for maintaining the country’s unity.
In a separate image Trump shared, he thanked Rutte after NATO’s boss described Trump’s achievements in Syria as “incredible”.

## SDF says it repelled 2 attacks by government forces in Kobani
The SDF says it repelled two attacks in the early hours of Tuesday carried out by government forces near the Syrian Kurdish town of Kobani.
It said in a statement that armoured military vehicles and tanks were destroyed and casualties were reported.
Fighters in the “south of Kobani witnessed two consecutive attack attempts by Damascus-affiliated factions, which were courageously and competently repelled by our forces”, it said.
“The confrontations on that front resulted in the destruction of a large number of their military vehicles and armoured vehicles, and the deaths of those inside them.”
The SDF took over control of Kobani after the armed group ISIL (ISIS) was driven from the city.

As part of its plan to unify the country following 14 years of brutal civil war, the Syrian government announced it had reached a [ceasefire agreement](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/1/18/syria-announces-ceasefire-agreement-with-kurd-led-sdf-after-heavy-fighting) with the Kurdish-led [Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF)](http://aljazeera.com/news/2019/10/15/who-are-the-syrian-democratic-forces) on Sunday. Under the agreement, the government will take over land held by the Kurdish armed group.
Despite the ceasefire, the Syrian army and the SDF reported [gun battles](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/liveblog/2026/1/19/syria-live-government-forces-take-control-after-heavy-fighting-with-sdf) in the country on Monday, in particular around a prison holding ISIL (ISIS) members in the town of al-Shadadi.

## What was agreed on Sunday?
President Ahmed al-Sharaa said the army would take control of three eastern and northeastern provinces – Raqqa, Deir Az Zor and Hasakah – from the SDF as part of the deal.
On Monday, a Ministry of Defence official said government-affiliated forces had arrived on the outskirts of the Kurdish-held city of Hasakah in the country’s northeast per this agreement.
The SDF is now to be integrated into Syria’s state institutions as part of a broader 14-point agreement.
Al-Sharaa’s government pledged to reunify Syria following the ousting of Bashar al-Assad in December 2024. On Friday, the president [issued a decree](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/1/17/syria-decree-grants-kurds-new-rights-formally-recognising-kurdish-language) declaring Kurdish a “national language” and granting the minority group official recognition.
“What [we] are witnessing now in the region is the end of the SDF,” Omar Abu Layla, a Syrian affairs analyst, told Al Jazeera.
The SDF in Syria represents the struggle of the Kurdish people, an ethnic group present across the Middle East.

Syria’s President Ahmed al-Sharaa is set to meet Mazloum Abdi, the head of the Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF), to [consolidate a ceasefire](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/1/18/syria-announces-ceasefire-agreement-with-kurd-led-sdf-after-heavy-fighting) after days of battles that saw the army and government-allied tribal forces sweep across the north of the country from Aleppo to Raqqa.
There have been renewed [deadly clashes](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/liveblog/2026/1/19/syria-live-government-forces-take-control-after-heavy-fighting-with-sdf) reported on Monday.
## Recommended Stories
list of 3 items
  * list 1 of 3[Recent fighting in Syria is “a nightmare scenario for the US”](https://www.aljazeera.com/video/quotable/2026/1/18/recent-fighting-in-syria-is-a-nightmare-scenario-for-the)
  * list 2 of 3[Syria announces ceasefire agreement with Kurd-led SDF after heavy fighting](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/1/18/syria-announces-ceasefire-agreement-with-kurd-led-sdf-after-heavy-fighting)
  * list 3 of 3[Will the Syrian army end up controlling Kurdish-held areas?](https://www.aljazeera.com/video/inside-story/2026/1/18/will-the-syrian-army-end-up-controlling-kurdish-held-areas)

end of list
The key meeting, postponed from Sunday due to bad weather, follows a dramatic escalation that saw Syrian government forces retake large swathes of territory in the northeast, forcing the SDF to accept a [truce](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/1/18/syrian-army-advances-on-sdf-stronghold-of-raqqa-whats-the-latest) and a wide-ranging agreement that brings Kurdish civilian and military authorities under central state control. Tensions remain high, with Kurdish-led forces reporting ongoing skirmishes with government forces on Monday.
On Sunday, Abdi, also known as Mazloum Kobani, announced his acceptance of the ceasefire declared and signed on camera by al-Sharaa, stating in a televised address that the conflict had been imposed on the SDF and planned by several parties. He said he would outline the terms of the agreement after returning from Damascus.
![Syria's President Ahmed al-Sharaa signs an agreement at the Presidential Palace in Damascus on January 18, 2026. Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa on January 18, announced an agreement with the chief of the Kurdish-led Syrian Democratic Forces that includes a ceasefire after government forces advanced in Kurdish-held areas of the north and east.](https://www.aljazeera.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/000_93679KJ-1768804765.jpg?w=770&resize=770%2C513&quality=80)Syria’s President Ahmed al-Sharaa signed an agreement in Damascus on January 18, 2026 [Rami al-Sayed/AFP]
Reporting from Damascus, Al Jazeera’s Ayman Oghanna said the ceasefire agreement triggered an outpouring of public joy in the capital.
“Last night, after the deal was struck, there were huge celebrations in the street – there were fireworks, there were cars honking, there were people dancing in the streets,” Oghanna said.
Advertisement 
He added that many expressed “a sense of relief and cautious joy, but some were sceptical” after weeks of deadly fighting.
That scepticism reflects fears that the ceasefire may not hold, Oghanna noted, in a country that was racked by a ruinous civil war for nearly 14 years.
The SDF accused the government of violating the fragile truce on Monday, reporting attacks on Shaddadi prison in Hasaka in northeast Syria, where former ISIL (ISIS) members are detained. The SDF also said that it was battling government forces near al-Aqtan prison outside of Raqqa, which it said is holding thousands of ISIL (ISIS) detainees.
The SDF warned that the skirmishes with government forces were a “highly dangerous development” that could “have serious security repercussions that threaten stability and pave the way for a return to chaos and terrorism”.

Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa on January 18, announced an agreement with the chief of the Kurdish-led Syrian Democratic Forces that includes a ceasefire after government forces advanced in Kurdish-held areas of the north and east.](https://www.aljazeera.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/000_93679KJ-1768804765.jpg?w=770&resize=770%2C513&quality=80)Syria’s President Ahmed al-Sharaa signed an agreement in Damascus on January 18, 2026 [Rami al-Sayed/AFP]
Reporting from Damascus, Al Jazeera’s Ayman Oghanna said the ceasefire agreement triggered an outpouring of public joy in the capital.
“Last night, after the deal was struck, there were huge celebrations in the street – there were fireworks, there were cars honking, there were people dancing in the streets,” Oghanna said.
Advertisement 
He added that many expressed “a sense of relief and cautious joy, but some were sceptical” after weeks of deadly fighting.
That scepticism reflects fears that the ceasefire may not hold, Oghanna noted, in a country that was racked by a ruinous civil war for nearly 14 years.
The SDF accused the government of violating the fragile truce on Monday, reporting attacks on Shaddadi prison in Hasaka in northeast Syria, where former ISIL (ISIS) members are detained. The SDF also said that it was battling government forces near al-Aqtan prison outside of Raqqa, which it said is holding thousands of ISIL (ISIS) detainees.
The SDF warned that the skirmishes with government forces were a “highly dangerous development” that could “have serious security repercussions that threaten stability and pave the way for a return to chaos and terrorism”.

[![](https://www.aljazeera.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/2017-06-08T131939Z_1189275883_RC11B0FE7F20_RTRMADP_3_GULF-QATAR-JAZEERA-1714943623.jpg?resize=96%2C96&quality=80)](https://www.aljazeera.com/author/al_jazeera_staff_150119130629458)
By [Al Jazeera Staff](https://www.aljazeera.com/author/al_jazeera_staff_150119130629458)
Published On 20 Jan 202620 Jan 2026

Tensions between the Syrian government and the Kurdish-led Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) have been escalating, days after they announced a ceasefire, [which is being severely tested](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/liveblog/2026/1/20/syria-fighting-live-ceasefire-under-duress-as-govt-moves-into-hasakah) by renewed fighting, involving the withdrawal of the latter’s forces from areas west of the Euphrates River.
Talks in Damascus between Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa and SDF leader Mazloum Abdi, also known as Mazloum Kobani, have faltered, and Abdi has returned to the northeast.
## Recommended Stories
list of 3 items
  * list 1 of 3[Will the Syrian army end up controlling Kurdish-held areas?](https://www.aljazeera.com/video/inside-story/2026/1/18/will-the-syrian-army-end-up-controlling-kurdish-held-areas)
  * list 2 of 3[Syrian president to meet SDF leader after ceasefire deal amid new clashes](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/1/19/syrias-president-al-sharaa-to-meet-sdf-leader-abdi-after-ceasefire-deal)
  * list 3 of 3[Power lines and power struggles: Unpacking Syria’s push towards unification](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/1/19/power-lines-and-power-struggles-unpacking-syria-push-towards-unification)

end of list
Contention and a blame game have surrounded the issues of ISIL (ISIS) prisoners who escaped from al-Shaddadi prison during the fighting between the army and the SDF. Syria’s Ministry of Interior said on Tuesday that 130 of 200 ISIL escapees had been recaptured.
The ministry accused the SDF of releasing ISIL fighters from the prison in the northeastern city of al-Shaddadi as a form of “political and security blackmail”. The army said it deliberately bypassed al‑Shaddadi prison in line with an agreement that the SDF would later hand control of the facility to authorities in Damascus.
The SDF has blamed the Syrian army for the prison break, saying it “lost control” of the jail after an attack by tribal fighters affiliated with the army.
SDF commander Fawza Youssef on Tuesday blamed al-Sharaa’s government for failing to uphold the deal.
“There is no political will on the part of the government to implement a ceasefire,” he told Al Jazeera. “If the violations and attacks continue, the SDF cannot be disarmed.”
The [agreement reached on Sunday](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/1/18/syria-announces-ceasefire-agreement-with-kurd-led-sdf-after-heavy-fighting) between al-Sharaa and Abdi stipulates the withdrawal of the SDF from Raqqa and Deir Az Zor, parts of which are east of the Euphrates, within a month.
Advertisement 
A spokesperson for the Interior Ministry accused the SDF of “trying to deflect blame” as it faced internal divisions.
“We prefer peaceful solutions, but all options are open,” the spokesperson told Al Jazeera.
notification-importantGet instant alerts and updates based on your interests. Be the first to know when big stories happen.
Yes, keep me updated
The deal included the SDF’s withdrawal from the area around the al-Aqtan prison. As it appeared on the verge of collapse, Al Jazeera correspondents on Tuesday reported that the Syrian army had begun artillery shelling around the prison and the SDF’s 17th Division headquarters in Raqqa.
Local sources in Hasakah province in the northeast said the army reached the Panorama intersection, located at the southern entrance to the provincial capital, also called Hasakah.
The Interior Ministry said it was ready to take over the management and security of ISIL prisons in Hasakah in accordance with international standards.
Information Minister Hamza Mustafa said the army secured the city of al-Shaddadi in the Hasakah countryside after taking control of the prison located there.
## Taking control of ISIL prisons
The SDF, the United States-backed force that fought ISIL in Syria, controls more than a dozen prisons in the northeast, where about 9,000 ISIL members have been held for years without trial.
While al-Sharaa’s government has pledged to reunify Syria after nearly 14 years of war, the SDF has repeatedly underlined al-Sharaa’s previous affiliation with Hayat Tahrir al-Sham (HTS), formerly part of al-Qaeda.
In a statement on Monday, the SDF referred to the government as “ISIS sympat

Syrian Democratic Forces fighters take part in a military parade in the US-protected al-Omar oilfield in the eastern province of Deir Az Zor on March 23, 2021 [Delil Soukeiman/AFP]
[![](https://www.aljazeera.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/SOR06401-1-1727077516.jpg?resize=96%2C96&quality=80)](https://www.aljazeera.com/author/sarah-shamim)
By [Sarah Shamim](https://www.aljazeera.com/author/sarah-shamim)
Published On 19 Jan 202619 Jan 2026

As part of its plan to unify the country following 14 years of brutal civil war, the Syrian government announced it had reached a [ceasefire agreement](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/1/18/syria-announces-ceasefire-agreement-with-kurd-led-sdf-after-heavy-fighting) with the Kurdish-led [Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF)](http://aljazeera.com/news/2019/10/15/who-are-the-syrian-democratic-forces) on Sunday. Under the agreement, the government will take over land held by the Kurdish armed group.
Despite the ceasefire, the Syrian army and the SDF reported [gun battles](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/liveblog/2026/1/19/syria-live-government-forces-take-control-after-heavy-fighting-with-sdf) in the country on Monday, in particular around a prison holding ISIL (ISIS) members in the town of al-Shadadi.

## What was agreed on Sunday?
President Ahmed al-Sharaa said the army would take control of three eastern and northeastern provinces – Raqqa, Deir Az Zor and Hasakah – from the SDF as part of the deal.
On Monday, a Ministry of Defence official said government-affiliated forces had arrived on the outskirts of the Kurdish-held city of Hasakah in the country’s northeast per this agreement.
The SDF is now to be integrated into Syria’s state institutions as part of a broader 14-point agreement.
Al-Sharaa’s government pledged to reunify Syria following the ousting of Bashar al-Assad in December 2024. On Friday, the president [issued a decree](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/1/17/syria-decree-grants-kurds-new-rights-formally-recognising-kurdish-language) declaring Kurdish a “national language” and granting the minority group official recognition.
“What [we] are witnessing now in the region is the end of the SDF,” Omar Abu Layla, a Syrian affairs analyst, told Al Jazeera.
The SDF in Syria represents the struggle of the Kurdish people, an ethnic group present across the Middle East.
## Who are the Kurds?
The Kurds are a group of people indigenous to the Mesopotamian plains and nearby highlands, which today stretch across southeastern Turkiye, northeastern Syria, northern Iraq, northwestern Iran and southwestern Armenia. The Kurdish population is concentrated in these areas, which are collectively referred to as Kurdistan.
Advertisement 
Kurds are, therefore, spread across several different countries in the Middle East and do not have a state of their own. They also have a large diaspora population, primarily in Germany but also in other European countries, including France, the Netherlands and Switzerland.
notification-importantGet instant alerts and updates based on your interests. Be the first to know when big stories happen.
Yes, keep me updated
There are 30 to 40 million Kurdish people around the globe. Kurds are widely understood to be the largest stateless ethnic group in the world, connected by a shared culture and the Kurdish language.
Kurdish, a northwestern Iranian language, has several distinct dialects that vary by region. Most historians agree that Kurds constitute the Iranian branch of the Indo‑European peoples.
While most Kurds are Sunni Muslims, there are also Kurdish communities that follow Shia Islam, Alevism, Yazidism, Christianity and other faiths.

![Mitsotakis calls for ‘logic and dialogue’ in EU-US relations](https://www.ekathimerini.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Mitsotakis_Tassoulas_Intime-960x600.jpg?v=1768902020)
[Yiannis Liakos/InTime News]
[ Newsroom ](https://www.ekathimerini.com/author/newsroom/ "View all posts by Newsroom")
January 20, 2026 20.01.2026 • 11:40
Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis has called for “logic and dialogue” to prevail in relations between the European Union and the United States, warning that such an approach is necessary to “avoid the worst” amid growing international tensions.
Mitsotakis made the remarks during his weekly meeting with President Konstantinos Tassoulas. Referring to Greece’s position on the international stage, he stressed that while the country maintains strategic relations with the United States, it is also firmly anchored within the European Union.
“Greece has strategic relations with the US, but it is also a member of the EU,” Mitsotakis said, adding that Athens has consistently worked to defend multilateralism and international law.
Addressing the broader global context, the prime minister said the world has entered 2026 facing unprecedented geopolitical and geoeconomic challenges, with long-standing assumptions that underpinned postwar peace and prosperity increasingly being called into question.
“In this environment, the interest of the homeland comes first,” Mitsotakis said, describing the strengthening of Greece’s armed forces and the country’s deterrent capacity as a national priority.
Mitsotakis’ remarks follow [comments he made on Monday](https://www.ekathimerini.com/politics/1292802/mitsotakis-says-changing-greenland-status-quo-would-be-disastrous/), in which he said that any attempt to change the status quo in Greenland, a self-governing territory of NATO member Denmark, would be “disastrous” and would constitute a “direct challenge to NATO.”
US President Donald Trump has caused widespread alarm by repeatedly threatening to take over Greenland. On Saturday, Trump announced a 10% import tax starting in February on goods from eight nations that have rallied around Denmark and Greenland.

![](https://greekcitytimes.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/greece-4-1170x600.jpg)
**Greece remains one of the top five travel destinations for Western European travellers over the next 12 months, according to the latest study by the Institute of the Association of Greek Tourism Enterprises (INSETE), reinforcing the country’s strong and steadily improving tourism brand.**
The study shows that Greece ranks third among preferred outbound travel destinations for travelers from Germany and Italy, fourth for France and the United Kingdom, and sixth for Spain. Compared with 2024, Greece improved its ranking by one position in both the UK and Spanish markets, while maintaining stable positions in the remaining markets. The UK and Germany continue to represent Greece’s two strongest inbound tourism markets in terms of both arrivals and revenue.
The findings come from INSETE’s January 2026 report titled _“Brand Greece: How Attractive Is Greece as a Tourist Destination? A Comparison with Competing Mediterranean Destinations.”_ The research focuses on key Western European markets, including Germany, France, the United Kingdom, Spain, and Italy. The underlying survey was conducted by GWI in two waves during 2025, involving 7,402 respondents, and also draws comparisons with data from two survey waves conducted in 2024. The study examines travel intentions for the next 12 months, including the crucial summer period for Greek tourism.
The markets analyzed account for nearly half of Greece’s inbound tourism revenue, contributing approximately 47–48% of total receipts, based on official Bank of Greece data for 2023–2024.
Across all surveyed markets, Greece places within the top five preferred destinations, ranking third or fourth in most cases, with the exception of Spain, where it ranks sixth. Even in the Spanish market, however, Greece improved its position by one place, confirming the resilience and appeal of the Greek tourism brand. Notably, the gap in preference between Greece and the country ranked immediately above it in both the UK and Spain is just two percentage points, highlighting Greece’s strong competitive momentum.
**Market comparisons for 2025 show the following:**
  * **Germany:** Greece ranks third with a preference rate of 19.7%, following Italy at 26.8% and Spain at 24.9%. Austria follows closely at 19.0%, while France records 17.9%.
  * **France:** Greece ranks fourth with 12.6%, behind Spain at 26.9%, Italy at 24.8%, and Portugal at 15.1%.
  * **United Kingdom:** Greece holds fourth place with a preference rate of 19.9%, after Spain, Italy, and France, while Portugal ranks sixth.
  * **Spain:** Greece ranks sixth with 9.5%, behind Italy, France, a

# Trump’s Gaza Board of Peace is concerning for Israeli security - opinion
## Israel risks being sidelined as Trump forms a controversial “Board of Peace” with potentially biased members.
![US President Donald Trump and Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. participate in a roundtable on rural health, at the White House in Washington, DC, US, January 16, 2026. ](https://images.jpost.com/image/upload/f_auto,fl_lossy/q_auto/c_fill,g_faces:center,h_720,w_1280/702371)US President Donald Trump and Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. participate in a roundtable on rural health, at the White House in Washington, DC, US, January 16, 2026. (photo credit: REUTERS/Nathan Howard)By[SHERWIN POMERANTZ](https://www.jpost.com/Author/Sherwin-Pomerantz)JANUARY 20, 2026 10:40
On October 28, 2018, a day after the massacre at Pittsburgh’s Tree of Life Congregation, I was in [New York](https://www.jpost.com/tags/new-york) on business and attended the annual meeting of the Tikva Foundation. I’m not on their political wavelength, but I was in the US, and they had a good lineup of speakers, so I went to hear them.
As the session opened, Chairman Emeritus Roger Hertog said that before the program began, thanks needed to be given to President Trump (remember, this was in his first term) for all he had done for [Israel](https://www.jpost.com/middle-east/article-883772). Everyone stood up and applauded… except for me.
One of the people at my table looked at me and asked why, since I live in Israel, I did not also stand up. I told her that, like all of us there, I was grateful for what he had done for Israel, but that I did not yet know what it would cost us.
Frankly, I was afraid that one day, when he no longer needs us, or at least perceives that he no longer needs us, he will throw us under the bus, because that’s just the way he operates. And, as expected, here we are, under the bus.
At the end of last week, the White House released the names of the people the president has invited to sit on the “Board of Peace,” which will be responsible for rebuilding the enclave and ensuring the disarmament of Hamas. Really? Let’s look at some of the people on the invitee list and see how committed they will be to the disarmament and elimination of Hamas.
![US President Donald Trump and Israeli President Benjamin Netanyahu walk together during Netanyahu's visit to Washington, February 5, 2025](https://images.jpost.com/image/upload/f_auto,fl_lossy/c_fill,g_faces:center,h_537,w_822/647874)US President Donald Trump and Israeli President Benjamin Netanyahu walk together during Netanyahu's visit to Washington, February 5, 2025 (credit: Avi Ohayon/GPO)
### **Canada's arms embargo on Israel**
Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney, who, in April of last year, let slip his stance on Israel policy when he responded to an anti-Israel heckler asking about the “genocide” in Gaza by saying, “I’m aware, that’s why we have an arms embargo,” to cheers of “Carney! Carney! Carney!” from the crowd.
Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan, who defined Hamas as a “liberation movement,” accused Israel of war crimes and spearheaded a campaign of blatant incitement based on distortions of fact, lies, and an anti-Israel and anti-Jewish narrative. Through his mouthpiece, the newspaper _Yeni Safak_ , the Turkish strongman calls Israel “Turkey’s number one enemy.”
Ali Al-Thawadi, a diplomat from Qatar, which has been a key mediator between Israel and Hamas throughout the war. Nevertheless, Qatar has a checkered history vis-à-vis Israel, having hosted the leadership of Hamas in luxurious quarters in Doha while Israel’s sons and daughters were being slaughtered on October 7, with others sacrificing their lives later in this far-too-long war.
Two other members of the Peace Board, Egypt’s General Intelligence Service chief Hassan Rashad and Gaza native Ali Shaath, head of the Palestinian committee tasked with managing the [Gaza](https://www.jpost.com/israel-news/defense-news/article-883815) Strip, met in Cairo this past Saturday to discuss humanitarian priorities and the delivery of aid. According to a report in the Egyptian Daily News, Shaath, Rashad, and members of the committee expressed appreciation for Egyptian efforts toward the Gaza Strip, specifically noting the stance of President Abdel Fattah al-Sisi regarding the prevention of Palestinian displacement and the preservation of the Palestinian cause.
Note the emphasis on the fact that Egypt, which could have made it possible for Gazans who wanted to enter the enclave during the war to do so, still does not want to accept any Arab Gazans into that country. During the meeting, they underscored the importance of opening all border crossings to allow the “largest possible amount” of humanitarian aid, subsistence materials, and necessary relief to reach residents, but reiterated their displeasure with the concept of Gazans leaving the strip through Egypt.
Sigrid Kaag, the Danish Interim

# Kurdish unity: Protests erupt across Iraq in support of Syria's Rojava 
## The crisis in Syria is affecting Kurds all over the world, primarily due to the fear that members of the Syrian government’s security forces may commit abuses amid the new offensive.
![Members of the Kurdish community and other protesters attend a demonstration against Syrian President Ahmed al‑Sharaa and recent military clashes between the Syrian army and Kurdish forces, in Berlin, Germany, January 19, 2026.](https://images.jpost.com/image/upload/f_auto,fl_lossy/q_auto/c_fill,g_faces:center,h_720,w_1280/702581)Members of the Kurdish community and other protesters attend a demonstration against Syrian President Ahmed al‑Sharaa and recent military clashes between the Syrian army and Kurdish forces, in Berlin, Germany, January 19, 2026.(photo credit: REUTERS/CHRISTIAN MANG)By[SETH J. FRANTZMAN](https://www.jpost.com/Author/Seth-J-Frantzman)JANUARY 20, 2026 10:25Updated: JANUARY 20, 2026 10:26
[Kurdish](https://www.jpost.com/middle-east/article-883838) protesters took to the streets across the Kurdistan Region of northern Iraq on Monday night amid increasing reports about clashes in Syria between the forces of the Syrian transitional government and those of the Kurdish-led [Syrian](https://www.jpost.com/middle-east/article-883886) Democratic Forces.
They were mobilizing support for the Kurdish region of Syria, known as Rojava in Kurdish. This display of Kurdish unity reminded some of other crises in recent memory in which Kurds were threatened.
The Kurdistan Region of northern Iraq is an autonomous region with its own government and armed forces. It is one area where Kurdish identity is officially celebrated, where people speak Kurdish, and where there is no authoritarian government that suppresses Kurdish rights. In other places, such as Iran and Turkey, Kurds may be arrested for demonstrating.
The crisis in Syria is affecting [Kurds](https://www.jpost.com/middle-east/article-883690) all over the world, primarily due to the fear that members of the Syrian government’s security forces may commit abuses amid the new offensive.
The Syrian government was supposed to come to an agreement with the SDF to integrate the SDF into the new security forces. However, neither side could agree on how to proceed.
![Members of the Kurdish community and other protesters hold a giant flag of Kurdistan, during a demonstration against Syrian President Ahmed al‑Sharaa and recent military clashes between the Syrian army and Kurdish forces, in front of the Brandenburg Gate, in Berlin, Germany, January 19, 2026.](https://images.jpost.com/image/upload/f_auto,fl_lossy/c_fill,g_faces:center,h_537,w_822/702582)Members of the Kurdish community and other protesters hold a giant flag of Kurdistan, during a demonstration against Syrian President Ahmed al‑Sharaa and recent military clashes between the Syrian army and Kurdish forces, in front of the Brandenburg Gate, in Berlin, Germany, January 19, 2026. (credit: REUTERS/CHRISTIAN MANG)
Despite a March 2025 agreement between Syrian transitional president Ahmed al-Shara’a and SDF leader Mazlum Abdi that created a roadmap for integrating the SDF and the forces in Damascus, there was no success in the integration.
Since March, the Syrian government has been on a roll, gaining international support and consolidating its power. The SDF has not been able to move forward with any clear plan for what to do next. The SDF is a close partner of the US in the war on ISIS; however, in November, Damascus also joined the coalition against ISIS.
The Syrian government decided at the end of 2025 that the time was up for the SDF to integrate. It launched an offensive to clear two Kurdish neighborhoods in Aleppo of SDF-linked security forces.
Later, the Syrian government set its sights on Dayr Hafir, an area near the Euphrates. The SDF, sensing its position was weakening, agreed to withdraw.
However, Damascus seized on the withdrawal as an opportunity to press its advantage and move forces across the Euphrates River, mobilizing tribes who have chafed under SDF rule.
On Monday, the SDF retreated even further, moving back towards Kurdish areas such as Kobane, Hasakah, and Qamishli. Now there is real fear that, as the Syrian government forces move forward, some of the elements of their forces may harm Kurds. Videos showing several dead Kurdish fighters, one with their throat appearing to be slit, have shocked people.
### **Murder of Kurdish activist Hevrin Khalaf by Turkish militia**
In the past, some of the Arab [Syrian](https://www.jpost.com/middle-east/article-883713) rebel militias in Syria have been known to kidnap Kurdish women and murder people. For instance, during the 2019 offensive by Turkey into Serekaniye in eastern Syria, the young female Kurdish activist Hevrin Khalaf was murdered by a militia backed by Ankara.
Some Turkish media celebrated her murder, depicting the unarmed woman activist as an enemy deserving of an extrajudic

# Syrian Kurds are fighting for survival – and Israel’s future is tied to theirs - opinion
## Northern Syria is collapsing into chaos, and unless the West acts, the Kurds will pay the price.
![Conoco gas plant after it came under the control of the Syrian government following the withdrawal of the US-backed Syrian Democratic Forces \(SDF\), in the countryside of Deir al-Zor, Syria, January 19, 2026.](https://images.jpost.com/image/upload/f_auto,fl_lossy/q_auto/c_fill,g_faces:center,h_720,w_1280/702458)Conoco gas plant after it came under the control of the Syrian government following the withdrawal of the US-backed Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF), in the countryside of Deir al-Zor, Syria, January 19, 2026.(photo credit: REUTERS/KHALIL ASHAWI)ByPAUSHALI LASSJANUARY 20, 2026 10:39
History repeats its patterns until those who ignore them are forced to confront the consequences. What is unfolding in northern [Syria](https://www.jpost.com/tags/syria) today is one of those moments.
Reports indicate Islamist detainees are being released from prisons in Deir ez-Zor, raising urgent concerns about a renewed jihadist threat. Deir ez-Zor is not just a strategic town on the Euphrates River in eastern Syria: It is part of a land steeped in history. The Euphrates marks the biblical border of the children of Abraham. This is the land where Abraham settled, amassed wealth and influence, and lived in coexistence with people of different faiths. The values he embodied – respect, dignity, coexistence – still shape the cultural DNA of the region.
Today, those same values, reflected in Kurdish society through respect for human dignity, women’s rights, and pluralism, are under direct threat from extremist ideologies that seek to destroy them.
Once again, it is the Kurds who are paying the price.
They have built governance systems that are pluralistic and comparatively stable, rare qualities in a region fractured by sectarianism and authoritarianism. They have fought on the front lines against ISIS at enormous cost. And each time geopolitical interests shift, they are abandoned. Their lives, their security, and their future are treated as expendable.
![Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa and Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan arrive for a joint press conference at the Presidential Palace in Ankara, Turkey, February 4, 2025.](https://images.jpost.com/image/upload/f_auto,fl_lossy/c_fill,g_faces:center,h_537,w_822/702383)Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa and Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan arrive for a joint press conference at the Presidential Palace in Ankara, Turkey, February 4, 2025. (credit: REUTERS/CAGLA GURDOGAN)
If the United States does not intervene decisively, the Syrian regime will not stop until Kurdish-held territory west of the Euphrates is severely weakened.
Meanwhile, the narrative that Abu Mohammad al-Julani has become a “legitimate partner” needs to be closely scrutinized by Western powers. Reports from Kurdish areas in Rojava (North and East Syria) describe widespread abuses under his command, even as al-Julani issues statements promising respect for Kurdish language, culture, and political expression.
Can he be trusted? His forces have been terrorizing civilians, desecrating bodies, and driving communities from their land. Whether he is orchestrating this or tolerating it is almost irrelevant. Leaders are judged by the actions of the forces they command.
For Israelis who lived through [October 7](https://www.jpost.com/tags/the-october-7-massacre), the similarities between the mass atrocities committed in Rojava against the Kurds and other non-Arab groups are strikingly familiar. This is not just physical violence but psychological warfare – white pickup trucks, armed men in threatening headbands, public humiliation, torture, women taken captive. The aim is to break a population’s will and force it from its land.
[Turkey](https://www.jpost.com/middle-east/article-883778) plays a central and deliberate role in this process. A full-scale invasion would spark international outrage, so [President Recep Tayyip Erdogan](https://www.jpost.com/tags/recep-tayyip-erdogan) operates through proxies in northern Syria. Turkey’s ambition is clear: destabilize the region, extend influence, and remake former Ottoman territories under its vision, all while hiding behind plausible deniability.
But this crisis does not end with the Kurds.
### **Living under the threat of annihilation**
The Kurds, like the Druze, are indigenous, non-Islamist minorities who stand in the way of extremist expansion. And they are natural allies of Israel. I know this not just as an analyst but personally. Since October 7, I have seen Kurdish individuals speak publicly and fearlessly in solidarity with Israel, often at serious personal risk. They understand what it means to live under the threat of annihilation, to face movements that deny a people’s right to exist in their ancestral homeland.
There is also a deeper resonance. Jews and Kurds are both

# Turkey
Turkey, officially the Republic of Turkey, is located partially in the continent of Asia and partially in Europe. It shares borders with Greece and Bulgaria to the Northwest, Iran to the east and Iraq to the southeast. It also shares various borders with Syria, Armenia, Azerbaijan and Georgia.
70%-75% of Turkey's population are Turks, and 19% are Kurds, making them the country's largest minority group. The country's capital is Ankara, and the largest city and financial city is Istanbul.
As of November 2021, the President of Turkey is Recep Tayyip Erdoğan, who has served in this position since 2014. Prior to that, he served as Prime Minister of the country from 2003-2014. 
Since the start of the Syrian civil war in 2011, an estimated 3.6 million Syrian refugees have taken up residence in Turkey, most of them living with temporary residence permits.

# Former PM Bennett: Enthusiastic Hamas aides, Turkey and Qatar, ushered through Gaza's front door
## “Erdogan and the despicable Qatar, the greatest haters of Israel, the most enthusiastic aides of Hamas, are being ushered in through the front door to run Gaza,” Bennett wrote on X/Twitter.
![Former Israeli prime minister Naftali Bennett attends a conference at the Academic College in Tel Aviv, January 7, 2026.](https://images.jpost.com/image/upload/f_auto,fl_lossy/q_auto/c_fill,g_faces:center,h_720,w_1280/702561)Former Israeli prime minister Naftali Bennett attends a conference at the Academic College in Tel Aviv, January 7, 2026.(photo credit: AVSHALOM SASSONI/FLASH90)By[SAM HALPERN](https://www.jpost.com/Author/sam-halpern)JANUARY 20, 2026 09:33Updated: JANUARY 20, 2026 09:51
[Former prime minister Naftali Bennett](https://www.jpost.com/tags/naftali-bennett) stated on Tuesday that the foremost Israel haters and supporters of Hamas, Turkey and Qatar, were being ushered through Gaza’s front doors via the US-led Gaza Board of Peace and slammed Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s government for allowing it.
“[Erdogan](https://www.jpost.com/middle-east/article-883778) and the despicable [Qatar](https://www.jpost.com/israel-news/defense-news/article-883803), the greatest haters of Israel, the most enthusiastic aides of Hamas, are being ushered in through the front door to run Gaza,” Bennett wrote on X/Twitter. “What a terrible diplomatic failure by the Netanyahu-Ben-Gvir-Smotrich government. Soon we will fix this, too.”
After the Board of Peace was announced on Friday, the Prime Minister’s Office released a statement the following day, objecting to it, asserting that it contradicts Israeli policy and was not brought up with Israeli authorities.
Among those named in the announcement was Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan. Israel has long objected to Turkish involvement in post-war Gaza, considering the issue a red line.
![Former prime minister Naftali Bennett and Foreign Minister Yair Lapid hold a joint press conference at the Israeli parliament in Jerusalem on June 20, 2022.](https://images.jpost.com/image/upload/f_auto,fl_lossy/c_fill,g_faces:center,h_537,w_822/682454)Former prime minister Naftali Bennett and Foreign Minister Yair Lapid hold a joint press conference at the Israeli parliament in Jerusalem on June 20, 2022. (credit: YONATAN SINDEL/FLASH90)
### **Board of Peace a 'diplomatic failure' for Israel**
Israeli politicians from across the spectrum, including opposition leader Yair Lapid, who called the US announcement a "diplomatic failure for Israel," subsequently came out against the board.
On Monday, [sources told Reuters](https://www.jpost.com/israel-news/article-883861) that Israel had also been invited to sit on the board. Nevertheless, Israel has yet to accept, and Netanyahu, in remarks made during the 40-signature debate in the Knesset plenum, [vowed that no Turkish or Qatari soldiers would be present](https://www.jpost.com/israel-news/article-883875) in Gaza.
“Turkish or Qatari soldiers will not be in the Strip,” the prime minister said. “We have a certain dispute with our friends in the United States regarding the composition of the advisory council that will accompany the processes in Gaza.”
